mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, mechanically lifting present valuations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 6.0%, it signals a tightening labor market that boosts household income and consumption demand, which translates into higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE drops below 6.0%, or the predictive Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The joint acceleration of housing starts and nonfarm payrolls indicates synchronized growth in construction activity and consumer spending, creating a positive earnings surprise cycle that lifts S&P 500 prices over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term in a multivariate regression yields a statistically insignificant coefficient (p > 0.10) over a 5-year out-of-sample test, or forward returns do not exceed the median when both series rise simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during late-cycle expansion reflects peak capacity utilization and pricing power, which supports corporate profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns turn negative when INDPRO rises above its 12-month moving average during late-cycle periods, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 50% in a 10-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: When quarterly real GDP growth exceeds 3.0%, it confirms robust aggregate demand expansion that directly supports corporate revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The hypothesis fails if 20-day forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero when GDPC1 growth surpasses 3.0%, or if the signal produces negative returns in more than 60% of triggered windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous federal funds rate hikes and accelerating CPI inflation compress real yields and trigger valuation multiple contraction, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when both FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL rise concurrently, or the interaction coefficient in a panel regression is positive and statistically significant over a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Rising residential construction financing levels indicate sustained household confidence and capital expenditure in the housing sector, which directly drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day SPX returns show no positive correlation with RSAFS increases, or the signal's information coefficient remains below 0.01 across all market regimes over a 10-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly payroll increase exceeding 200,000 jobs signals strong labor demand that boosts disposable income and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative or flat 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances where PAYEMS exceeds the 200k threshold, or the predictive Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.
